summary: Joel Spolsky promotes Seth Godin's keynote from the 2008 Business of Software conference, highlighting Godin's insight that mass-market advertising leads to mediocre products. He emphasizes Godin's reputation as a transformative speaker whose seminars command premium prices and sell out instantly. The post shares a link to watch the full hour-long talk online.
key_points: Mass-market advertising forces companies toward average products for average people
Seth Godin's speaking ability commands premium pricing ($1650) with instant sellouts
The Business of Software conference made Godin's keynote available for free online
money_quotes: If you're going to interrupt everybody with an ad, it better be something everybody wants to buy. So what do you end up with? Average products for average people.
key_insight: Mass-market advertising pushes companies toward building average products for average people rather than remarkable products for specific audiences.
